reviews.review_description:
I'm really enjoying this game! Once you get on it's hard to stop and I find myself losing track of time. There is really so much to do! Hours and hours of fun and lots of areas yo unlock and explore.    Its very immersive and I love to escape to my own little island. Nice game for all ages and just lots of relaxing fun!

UPDATE:  They finally updated this game about a year ago and its so much better now.  The fishing is a bit easier, they've sharpened up the graphics,  and added a teleport option finally! Perfect for people that enjoyed the game but could not play it previously due to motion sickness. You can now turn off those pesky raccoons for a more peaceful experience. Oh! And we got a new Nym suit for our avatars!
I love the game, but I can't upgrade some of it because the spices don't work correctly when adding to the recipes. Please fix this..

I found this game after playing Garden of the Sea recently and am really enjoying it so far but am having trouble with adding the spices to recipes at the restaurant. Whenever I add the spices to the foods (as the last ingredient) it doesn’t seem to be registering the addition and the food item is the “plain” version of it. I can’t create the food item to purchase the clothes for the chef, so I’m stuck, is there anyway to update this on developer end?

I freaking love this new update! No more raccoons coming to steal my things!😊 You now have the option to turn the raccoons off or on.👈 The only thing I wish this game had was more fruit instead of just one on the trees.☹️maybe add more areas to explore. I hope they add an option to control the day and night cycles . 🤔Keep up the good work, guys! I'm so glad they are still updating this game. Sending Positive Vibes their way. I hope all are having a great day. Please stay safe.🙏🏻🫡😎
I absolutely love this game it reminds me of animal crossing and stardew valley but for vr. definitely recommend if you like either of those games to get this.
It's good if you are looking for a chilled game to play, just helping animals on the island, fishing, mining etc.

I love VR but I have struggled to find any VR game that my wife would enjoy. This game is the first VR game that she tried loved. She has been playing this every day anytime she has a free moment. She says it’s like Animal Crossing and Stardew Valley. And the cooking reminds her of Zelda. This has an incredible art style and is so pretty. I really enjoy watching her explore and play because the art style and colors are so good. I highly recommend this as an awesome VR cosy game.
I love this game.

This game is essentially a cozy lifestyle game similar to the likes of animal crossing sharing some similarities, if you enjoy cozy games that are laidback and keep you occupied for hours then definitely give this a try its very fun and enjoyable

reviews.review_title: The only app I have with glitches
reviews.review_description: I would love this game, but I will be playing and I suddenly get taken to a area where I’m stuck. Animals walking upside down and objects frozen mid air. Only way to get out is power down. I want to play for enjoyment not for frustration.

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear you are having trouble.  Please come to our discord for support:

 https://discord.gg/hiddenpath

reviews.review_description:
I’m always on the lookout for relaxing games I can play with my girlfriend. When I found this game it seemed to have the whole package - Farming, Crafting, Building etc, all the stuff my gf enjoys with the added bonus of cute animal NPCs.

**The Good Stuff**
The general feel of this game is really nice. The environment and music make for a really relaxing setting. Love the adorable NPCs that you can interact with and even pet. The narration is also well done. If you like grinding and collecting recourses in games, this definitely scratches that itch. The art style is really well implemented as well. 
Crafting is done through specific NPCs which is good or bad depending on what type of player you are (Similar to Enshrouded’s crafting system). If you’re a casual gamer that doesn’t enjoy complex crafting, mini games (like fishing) or tedious sub-crafting systems like repairing equipment, this is the laid back game for you.

**Now for the Bad Stuff**
As I mentioned, I bought this for me and my girlfriend to play on each of our Quest headsets. Unfortunately, CoOp gameplay is pretty much unplayable in our short experiences. Tried a few times and ran into the same problems each time.
The main issues that turned me away from this game were mainly because of the lack of importance for the joining player(s). I started with joining my gf’s world to start. The first issue/bug I noticed was that for some reason I couldn’t collect Twine, nor take out any Twine from my backpack that I collected in my world. But the big issue is that I was basically just felt like another NPC in her game. The narrator didn’t talk to me, I couldn’t interact with the buildings or use the Hearts in any way, and I couldn’t interact with/fulfil any of NPC’s requests after my gf had already interacted with them first. This was a dealbreaker for me as we were looking forward to something akin to a Stardew Valley experience in VR. 

Some minor things to note if the devs read this as well: 
- Whenever one of us removed our headset before resuming the game, we found we had to close the game entirely and reopen before being able to see and join each other’s worlds. 
- Please review the outer regions of the map. Namely the farm area - I went to do some sea fishing over the cliff behind the barn and there seems to be no fish there. Also I noticed the edge of the map to the right side, behind the boulder, might be worth filling that in to save on immersion. 
- There really ought to be more indication of, or a subtler transition between changes of the time of day. When it changes, it suddenly fades to black over about 3 seconds and anything you were doing is interrupted/reset.

**TL;DR**
It’s a really nice concept and it can be really relaxing to play Solo, the NPCs are cute, the environment is colourful and varied, and the music and tone is super chill/relaxing and beautifully done.
Unfortunately I’d have to advise anyone looking for a chill co-op game to avoid until the issues with multiplayer are fixed - at the very least, there needs to at least be an option for friends to have the same level of interaction in the world, as in its current state it’s just not fun to play with friends.

Will be requesting a refund for now, but will definitely keep an eye on this one for future updates as it has amazing potential. Please keep working on this devs, it has the foundations for an iconic VR experience if the Co-Op issues are dealt with properly.

I bought this game ages ago but didn't think of it much back then, but now-wow! 

With all the graphic updates  this game looks great with a magical feel to it. I'm finding myself lost for hours playing raccoon lagoon the time just flies by.

I truly recommend it, and it should be selling for a higher price in my opinion, at a price of £10 pounds in October 2024 this game is true value for money!

This game is basically broken and unsupported by devs. I saw this game when it came out, looked great to play with my god daughter, she now has a quest so we got it. I've spent hours trying to get the 2 of us to be connected and in the same world as each other and after all that trying (and now probably longer than 2 hrs so I can't get my money back) I've come to the conclusion the game is so full of bugs it's unusable as advertised! You either keep getting kicked out of each others worlds or struggle to find each other etc etc. It's so frustrating because it would and could be great with the younger ones but alas after having been out for so long and it still being in this state tells you all you need to know about the support of it. I saw someone say they couldn't understand all the bad reviews..... Well that makes sense if they played it as single player, but if ya after multiplayer forget it.

It's almost bordering on me putting in an official complaint as its from oculus themselves. It's fine saying you can get your money back if you've played for under 2hrs, but the problem is if you've spent over 2hrs trying to make the game do what its advertising itself as being able to do without any success then surely legally I have some right to request a refund despite the time 'played' on it. Of the time I and my god child played on it, 80% of the time was either being kicked off the game or trying to connect to each other. I've bought a lot of games and never had such a bad experience as this one.

So I've read the response about 3 years after I bought 2 copies of this game which pretty much validates my original review. So I thought ok let's give it another go, 3 years too late or not. Is connecting to another player better? Yes, 100%, but we now have the new problem, one player now can't get the rod as it's not possible to pick up rope! Nor can that player store or use pick axe's, and that's some of the bugs, I've just stuck
 to game breaking rather than listing all the others. Just to add insult to injury I have been provided a link to discord that links to no discord channel..... Fitting.

reviews.review_description: I bought this game over a year ago.  I stopped using my VR for a few months and now it won't load.  Crashes to my oculus home screen after the picture with all the critters in it.  I can not find any info anywhere on how to fix this.  I have Uninstalled and reinstalled 3 times. VERY disappointed as this was a complete waste of money.  If you are thinking of purchasing it, save your money.

reviews.review_title: So much more than initially appears
reviews.review_description:
At first it seemed like a pretty simple game, and admittedly it kind of is. But I, a grown man, managed to spend 200 hours in it so far, lol. I have technically finished it, but like all games of this style, the game being finished is more a matter of opinion.

I have played and loved games like minecraft and harvest moon, this may not be quite as elaborate as those, but it has a good mix of those elements, and what it does have works well for it. I do think eventually someone will out do this style of game in the VR space, but at the moment it's the best one.

There are some downsides, especially on the Quest or Quest 2. When animals get mad, rightfully so, they leave a little poop behind as punishment. Which honestly is a great mechanic, and initially the fact that poops can not be gotten rid of feels right. But on the Quest and Quest 2, if you have kids playing in your game, those poops can add up pretty fast. And they have some real weight. I have my own relatively clean save and that game runs flawlessly, but the kids save crashes and runs quite poorly alot of the time. The assumption I am making is that them having literally hundreds of poops is really bogging everything down. They have since grown up and play the game more responsibly and have learnt the lesson the poop system was designed to teach them. But now they have the dilemma of whether they live with their world running so poorly forever, or chuck out 2 years of building and designing their unique island.

My proposal would be a system to use hearts to clean up poop. I don't know if anyone is still in charge of working on this game, it came out quite a while ago, but if you guys can still make little changes here and there, the girls would gladly spend hundreds of hearts to make their town run better, and smell better. The nyms have all forgiven them for their earlier ways by now, I'm sure.

Might also be a good idea to have a way to see the performance impact of decorations, and maybe a little red bit of the bar for how much the poop is costing you, hehe. 

And if you anyone is still working on the game, maybe another use for hearts in the end-game could be placating the Racoons. Pay them some hearts to make them feel better and convert them to normal animals, with quests where they want random food items and they'll give random decorations or something. My nieces always want to be nice to the Racoons, so they normally just let them steal food to feed their families, but stop them if they try to steal anything else.

Anyway, it's a pretty great little game. Helped teach the nieces some good stuff, looking forward to playing multiplayer with them since they will have a headset at their house soon.

developer_response.body: Come check out the Racconiversary update we’ve fixed many of the bugs and added some quality of life features (including no bandits!).  You can let us know your thoughts on discord: https://discord.com/channels/385613412455153667/580116010716102666

reviews.review_description: I haven’t been able to play this game once. It crashes every single time. And since it was a bundle I cannot get a refund. No response on discord to game issues. I’m not the only one experiencing this issue so buyer beware
Don't know what I was expecting. Think target audience are toddlers.
It's bad
Would recommend this game, so fun and enjoyable can play forever. I get motion sick really fast but fun.

reviews.review_title: Love it but i have a problem.
reviews.review_description: So. This game is adorable and fun and I love the concept of this! But there’s one problem which might just be a problem I have and not the games, but I don’t know. Whenever I move my eyes hurt because I’m not actually moving. Yet again. I don’t know if this is a problem only I have.

developer_response.body: Come check out the Racconiversary update! We have added a teleport move option to help those who experienced motion sickness.

reviews.review_title: Very cute and fun to play :)
reviews.review_description: I enjoy the speed that the game progresses. It’s a slower paced cozy game or you can grind and play it quickly if you would rather. The way the creatures move is a bit silly looking but I think it makes the game more unique and fun to play and enjoy the beautiful locations within the map of the island. I gave it 4 stars because the game can be a bit confusing with what the creatures are asking for and what resources you have at hand.  Overall worth the money!

So far I love this game. It keeps you busy and there is plenty to do. The only small things I wish they would add is maybe a mini map…and then when you grow crops let 3 grow at a time instead of harvesting only 1 at a time. Just a thought. Other than that I have no reason not to like this game. It’s great.

Have played this game a couple of hours and had a lot of fun so far. It's a bit like Animal Crossing - very cute character you can pet and bring gifts. Harvest resources by chopping wood or planting crops. And help build up a village. Motion sickness was not too bad for me.

I was hoping to find a wholesome survival/farming game to play on here and this one seems to be one I can enjoy! It has cute little animals you have to help and they react to you petting or throwing them lol. The multiplayer is kinda cool too, people can visit and help out a bit which is nice. There are some moments of freezing or glitching but I feel like thats to be expected. If youre into any games like animal crossing/minecraft/stardew I feel like this has a little of each of those characteristics in the game.
I really hope to see this game get more updates, with the right amount of development I could see Raccoon Lagoon being a ridiculously popular game for VR.
I have only been able to play for a few hours here and there since my download but I am enjoying it immensely. Unlocking new areas and helping the new inhabitants is addicting.

My review used to be a really long winded 3 star, but considering I haven’t touched the game in two years it’s kinda just faded into obscurity for me.

People explain it incorrectly as an animal crossing esque game, but nothing is nearly as close. I honestly think they’ve never played animal crossing.

Items don’t despawn, you can clip through the map floor, it has no beneficial mechanics at all, but of everything at least it’s charming.

When visiting a multiplayer world they don’t get an option to reject you, so I maxed out everything the game in a matter of hours by visiting other’s worlds and stealing items… then leaving

reviews.review_title: I want to love this game…
reviews.review_description: I want to love this game but the walk style is horrible. I wish they had an option to teleport like most VR games. Sliding around is really disorienting for most people.

developer_response.body: Come check out the Racconiversary update! We have added a teleport move option to help those who were feeling sick when moving.

reviews.review_title: Super fun and tons of stuff to do
reviews.review_description:
This is a review from an average gamer.  If you like Animal Crossing and Harvest Moon, this game has combined elements.

You get to gather resources by doing anything from finding them on the ground to farming them, chopping trees, mining, fishing, trading with the animals, etc.

The game does a great job of introducing you to the different elements gradually so you don't get overwhelmed.

Handling objects is done very well, managing inventory is wonderful with the tablet, the day/cycle really regulates the gameplay. 

As for the two big claims, motion sickness and glitches... The game runs smooth and I don't think the motion is anymore intense than something like Rec Room or any other game with artificial locomotion.  In fact, I found it quite the opposite, this was a great game to start with getting used to VR motion because it's more relaxed. But everybody is different.

As for glitches, I haven't noticed anything major, and definitely not anything that caused any hiccups in my game play. 

All in all, I'm loving this game and can't seem to put it down.  There's a ton to do, lots to interact with, and extremely fun to play.  It's well worth the price!

The only thing I cannot comment on is the multiplayer since I don't heave anyone to play it with online.

I recommend this for sure!
I like this game as I’m 65 and just wanted a low action adventure game.  Fun to play.
I love this game but can no longer play due to the fact that I’m stuck in the floor and can’t move!! Resetting my guardian doesn’t help idk what to do :(
